title: Show empty values in axis on charts

posts.author: Lucyj1983 (Lucy)
posts.cookedHtml:
Description

Show charts with the empty values along the x-axis.

What are you trying to achieve

It would be so helpful visually to see empty values along the x-axis in charts. We ask scale of 1-10 questions and visually seeing the range of scores across the x-axis would be so helpful. That way if all scores are showing at the right side of the chart we know we’re doing well, if all scores are showing at the left side of the chart we need to do something different!

posts.author: Lucyj1983 (Lucy)
posts.cookedHtml: We use the charts to show the responses from training evaluation forms. We score on a scale of 1-10, 1 being not good 10 being excellent. Having the non-answered labels on the x axis is an incredibly useful visual as at a glance we can see if we are scoring high or low.

posts.author: Lucyj1983 (Lucy)
posts.cookedHtml: No it would need to be along the x-axis

posts.author: johnelia543 (John Elia)
posts.cookedHtml:
Lucyj1983:

We use the charts to show the responses from training evaluation forms. We score on a scale of 1-10, 1 being not good 10 being excellent Having the non-answered labels on the x axis is an incredibly useful visual as at a glance we can see if we are scoring high or low.

@Lucyj1983 oh, now i understand and 1 is the low value and 10 is the excellent value. and also tell me is thsi topic also almost the same: Is there a way to have empty dates populate on the xaxis?

posts.author: Lucyj1983 (Lucy)
posts.cookedHtml: Unfortunately no. It’s really quite a simple thing and hopeful Monday.com will implement it. We have moved from Survey Monkey who’s charts show the empty value columns on the axis. Sadly Monday.com’s version of charts is just not as visuablyy impactful as Survey Monkey.
